title: CloudFront Origins, OAC, SSE-KMS, and Private Delivery · Runtime Contract: Oac Signing Behavior
summary: A distribution points at an S3 website endpoint, retains legacy OAI by habit, permits the CloudFront service principal globally, and forgets the KMS key policy. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the oac signing behavior increment in private CloudFront-to-S3 origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Oac Signing Behavior · Theory Oac Signing Behavior
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 7091 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Oac Signing Behavior contract for the supplied data.
